From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American retired actor (born 1930)

This article is about the actor. For the software developer, see [Alan B. Oppenheimer](/wiki/Alan_B._Oppenheimer "Alan B. Oppenheimer").

| --- | --- |

| Born | (1930-04-23) April 23, 1930 (age 95)  [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City"), U.S. |

**Alan Oppenheimer** (born April 23, 1930) is an American retired actor. He has performed numerous roles on live-action television since the 1960s and has had an active career doing voice work since the 1970s.

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Alan_Oppenheimer&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

Oppenheimer was born in [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City") on April 23, 1930, to Louis and Irene Oppenheimer. His father was a stockbroker.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Alan_Oppenheimer&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

### Character roles

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Alan_Oppenheimer&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Character roles")]

As a character actor, Oppenheimer has had diverse roles in popular American television programming, from playing a [Nazi](/wiki/Nazi "Nazi") and a Hollywood movie star on *[Hogan's Heroes](/wiki/Hogan%27s_Heroes "Hogan's Heroes")*, to playing an Israeli secret agent as well as a double-agent KAOS scientist on *[Get Smart](/wiki/Get_Smart "Get Smart")*, to being the second actor to play Dr. Rudy Wells in *[The Six Million Dollar Man](/wiki/The_Six_Million_Dollar_Man "The Six Million Dollar Man")* ([Martin Balsam](/wiki/Martin_Balsam "Martin Balsam") played the role in the pilot film). Oppenheimer took over as Rudy starting with the second pilot film, *Wine, Women and War*, up until the introduction of *[The Bionic Woman](/wiki/The_Bionic_Woman "The Bionic Woman")* in 1975, whereupon [Martin E. Brooks](/wiki/Martin_E._Brooks "Martin E. Brooks") took over as Wells up until cancellation. He was the original Mickey Malph ([Ralph Malph](/wiki/Ralph_Malph "Ralph Malph")'s dad) on *[Happy Days](/wiki/Happy_Days "Happy Days")*. He played a recurring role during the first two seasons of *[St. Elsewhere](/wiki/St._Elsewhere "St. Elsewhere")* as Helen Rosenthal's husband, [Ira](/wiki/List_of_St._Elsewhere_characters "List of St. Elsewhere characters"). He had a recurring role as Mayor Alvin B. Tutwiller on *[Mama's Family](/wiki/Mama%27s_Family "Mama's Family")*.

Oppenheimer has appeared in science fiction in the 1973 cult classic *[Westworld](/wiki/Westworld_(film) "Westworld (film)")*, where he played the head IT technician. He has also appeared in three *[Star Trek](/wiki/Star_Trek "Star Trek")* series, always playing a different character. He appeared in the *[Star Trek: The Next Generation](/wiki/Star_Trek:_The_Next_Generation "Star Trek: The Next Generation")* episode "[Rightful Heir](/wiki/Rightful_Heir "Rightful Heir")" as a [Klingon](/wiki/Klingon "Klingon") cleric, Koroth, a primary instigator of the [cloning](/wiki/Cloning "Cloning") of [Kahless](/wiki/Kahless "Kahless"); on *[Deep Space Nine](/wiki/Star_Trek:_Deep_Space_Nine "Star Trek: Deep Space Nine")* as [Starfleet](/wiki/Starfleet "Starfleet") [Captain](/wiki/Captain_(naval) "Captain (naval)") [Declan Keogh in command of the *USS Odyssey*](/wiki/The_Jem%27Hadar "The Jem'Hadar"); and as an alien ambassador in *[Voyager](/wiki/Star_Trek:_Voyager "Star Trek: Voyager")*.

Oppenheimer also appeared as film director [Cecil B. DeMille](/wiki/Cecil_B._DeMille "Cecil B. DeMille") in the 1994 Broadway production of [Andrew Lloyd Webber](/wiki/Andrew_Lloyd_Webber "Andrew Lloyd Webber")'s *[Sunset Blvd.](/wiki/Sunset_Boulevard_(musical) "Sunset Boulevard (musical)")*[[3]](#cite_note-3)

### Voice acting

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Alan_Oppenheimer&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Voice acting")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/22/Loren_Lester_05.jpg/250px-Loren_Lester_05.jpg)](/wiki/File:Loren_Lester_05.jpg)

Oppenheimer (left) with [Jim Cummings](/wiki/Jim_Cummings "Jim Cummings") (middle) and [Loren Lester](/wiki/Loren_Lester "Loren Lester") (right) in 2015

Oppenheimer has voiced many characters, often for [Filmation](/wiki/Filmation "Filmation") in the 1970s and 1980s, such as Fraidy Cat, Jasper Catdaver, Captain Eddie Kittenbacker and Cloud Nine on *[Uncle Croc's Block](/wiki/Uncle_Croc%27s_Block "Uncle Croc's Block")*'s *[Fraidy Cat](/wiki/Fraidy_Cat_(TV_series) "Fraidy Cat (TV series)")*; [Mighty Mouse](/wiki/Mighty_Mouse "Mighty Mouse"), Oil Can Harry, Swifty and the narrator on *[The New Adventures of Mighty Mouse and Heckle & Jeckle](/wiki/The_New_Adventures_of_Mighty_Mouse_and_Heckle_%26_Jeckle "The New Adventures of Mighty Mouse and Heckle & Jeckle")*; [Ming the Merciless](/wiki/Ming_the_Merciless "Ming the Merciless") on *[Flash Gordon](/wiki/The_New_Adventures_of_Flash_Gordon "The New Adventures of Flash Gordon")*; the Overlord on *[BlackStar](/wiki/BlackStar "BlackStar")*; [Skeletor](/wiki/Skeletor "Skeletor"), Man-At-Arms and [Mer-Man](/wiki/Mer-Man "Mer-Man") from Filmation's 1980s cartoon *[He-Man and the Masters of the Universe](/wiki/He-Man_and_the_Masters_of_the_Universe "He-Man and the Masters of the Universe")*; and the voice of Prime Evil in the 1986 TV series, Filmation's *[Ghostbusters](/wiki/Ghostbusters_(1986_TV_series) "Ghostbusters (1986 TV series)")*.[[4]](#cite_note-voice-4) Other notable voice roles include *[Thundarr the Barbarian](/wiki/Thundarr_the_Barbarian "Thundarr the Barbarian")*, Vanity on *[The Smurfs](/wiki/The_Smurfs_(1981_TV_series) "The Smurfs (1981 TV series)")*, Rhinokey and Crock from *[The Wuzzles](/wiki/The_Wuzzles "The Wuzzles")* and Falkor, [Gmork](/wiki/Gmork "Gmork"), Rockbiter, and the Narrator from 1984's *[The NeverEnding Story](/wiki/The_NeverEnding_Story_(film) "The NeverEnding Story (film)")*. In the early 1990s, Oppenheimer was the voice of [Merlin](/wiki/Merlin "Merlin") in *[The Legend of Prince Valiant](/wiki/The_Legend_of_Prince_Valiant "The Legend of Prince Valiant")*. He also provided the voice of Barkerville in the *[Pound Puppies](/wiki/Pound_Puppies_(TV_special) "Pound Puppies (TV special)")* TV special, and provided additional voices on *[Battle of the Planets](/wiki/Battle_of_the_Planets "Battle of the Planets")* in 1978.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/57/Loren_Lester_06.jpg/250px-Loren_Lester_06.jpg)](/wiki/File:Loren_Lester_06.jpg)

Oppenheimer and Loren Lester posing for a 2015 photograph

Oppenheimer worked on *[The Transformers](/wiki/The_Transformers_(TV_series) "The Transformers (TV series)")*, most notably as two contrasting characters, the [pacifist](/wiki/Pacifist "Pacifist") [Beachcomber](/wiki/Beachcomber_(Transformers) "Beachcomber (Transformers)") and the bellicose [Warpath](/wiki/Warpath_(Transformers) "Warpath (Transformers)"). His rendition of [Seaspray](/wiki/Seaspray_(Transformers) "Seaspray (Transformers)") was remarkably similar to [Mer-Man](/wiki/Mer-Man "Mer-Man"), including the gurgling effects. He took over the voice of [Roger Smith](/wiki/Characters_of_The_Big_O#Roger_Smith "Characters of The Big O")'s butler [Norman Burg](/wiki/Characters_of_The_Big_O#Norman_Burg "Characters of The Big O") in the English dub of the second season of *[The Big O](/wiki/The_Big_O "The Big O")*. He was the voice of the never-seen Alistair Crane on the [soap opera](/wiki/Soap_opera "Soap opera") *[Passions](/wiki/Passions "Passions")* until 2004, when the character was finally shown on camera, played by David Bailey. More recently, he provided the voice of the scientist for the 2009 film *[9](/wiki/9_(2009_animated_film) "9 (2009 animated film)")* and Batman's butler [Alfred Pennyworth](/wiki/Alfred_Pennyworth "Alfred Pennyworth") in *[Superman/Batman: Public Enemies](/wiki/Superman/Batman:_Public_Enemies "Superman/Batman: Public Enemies")*.

Oppenheimer's repertoire also includes [video games](/wiki/Video_game "Video game"), voicing Dr. Piotr Ivanovich in *[Soldier of Fortune II: Double Helix](/wiki/Soldier_of_Fortune_II:_Double_Helix "Soldier of Fortune II: Double Helix")*, [Prometheus](/wiki/Prometheus "Prometheus") in *[God of War II](/wiki/God_of_War_II "God of War II")* and Jandor the Airship Captain in *[Nox](/wiki/Nox_(video_game) "Nox (video game)")*. He additionally voiced Harold in *[Fallout: Brotherhood of Steel](/wiki/Fallout:_Brotherhood_of_Steel "Fallout: Brotherhood of Steel")*, Dyntos and the Chariot Master in *[Kid Icarus: Uprising](/wiki/Kid_Icarus:_Uprising "Kid Icarus: Uprising")*, and Darm and the Narrator in *[Ys](/wiki/Ys_(series) "Ys (series)")*.

In 2019, Oppenheimer guest-starred on the animated series *[Tigtone](/wiki/Tigtone "Tigtone")* and featured in *[Toy Story 4](/wiki/Toy_Story_4 "Toy Story 4")* as Old Timer.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Alan_Oppenheimer&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Oppenheimer married costume designer Marianna Elliott in 1958 and together they had three children.[[6]](#cite_note-Variety-6) The couple divorced, but they remarried in 1992 and they remained married until her death in 2003.[[6]](#cite_note-Variety-6) In March 2025, Oppenheimer was unable to attend a local convention because he contracted [pneumonia](/wiki/Pneumonia "Pneumonia").[[7]](#cite_note-7)

## Filmography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Alan_Oppenheimer&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Filmography")]
